review_text: I very much enjoyed the 30+ hours I've put into this, I really....REALLY have, but this is not a game. This is a DLC pack for Dying Light 2 that they shoved a bunch of filler into. It's fun, and any fan of the games is going to enjoy it, but I can't recommend it at it's current price point.

Game just doesn't feel fun, Zombies are to tanky, weapons are made of toilet paper, animation locks from zombies happen from like 5 feet away, It might get better but first impression is a pretty hard miss. So finished the game on Burtal, and it just wasn't very fun, the mechanics were shallow you never felt strong, the game just feels like a DLC, honestly if the game was a DLC it would be decent but you can't pass this off as a full game.

Love Dying Light 1 and I wasn't a huge fan of Dying Light 2. This game originally was meant to be a DLC and within the first two hours of gameplay I could notice that. It's not a bad game by any means ripping people apart is awesome but the price definitely isn't worth it, I could notice from the skill tree and the progression very early on that this game would be extremely short. It is definitely an upgrade from the original and dying light 2, but its essentially just the same as Spider Man: Miles Morales where you're getting a pretty short experience for a game that is worth 60 dollars. I would definitely buy this if it was 40 dollars or less though.

The Grab mechanic the zombies have ruins this game. they grab way too often, and it even cancels you if you are mid swing with your melee attack. once it even happend when i used the charge kick on a zombie and it just grabbed me any way, like i didnt even do it in the first place... suuuper annoying.

They make you travel five minutes to talk to someone for two seconds, then travel five minutes back to where you just came from for the previous quest, kill a few zombies, and rinse and repeat that silly loop for 80% of the game.

not sure how this game got such glowing reviews. its more dying light, but it's lost the charm the first game had. its very repetitive, combat start to get monotonous, and zombies non stop grapple. i'd give it a 5.5/10

For 60 dollars, you're getting: a DLC rushed into a full game with very little effort. Its literally just dying light 2 with less features, less exploration, and less variety. Yay Crane is back, filled with plot holes! Yay cars are back, but they're slow, made of cardboard, and completely useless with all the trees in the way! Yay guns are back and with all the ammo you get, even on brutal, why use anything else! Unless its on a sale i wouldn't recommend it. The fact that there are area levels alone should tell you how i feel about this open world zombie survival parkour exploration game. Literally just play dying light 1 or 2.

I just started the game and it has already spammed me 3 times and opened the browser to create an account on the EpiG shit website, what kind of shady ads is this. The story takes place in the Alps in Europe but 99% of the zombies are black.

Reused a ton of assets then charging $60 for it, then cut and resold content which was not included for buying the ultimate edition prior (I can overlook this but I'm not ignoring it). Combat is somehow less responsive than DL2, and parkour feels float-y and slow. Skill tree is extremely simplified as they give you previous skills at the start, but don't fill the gaps so it's barren. Can't sit here and bark that it'll get ironed out, because it shouldn't be happening again, especially for a dlc priced at a triple A game price.

Game runs smooth, similar combat feeling to dl1/2, parkour feels less floaty as well. the beast is fun, but would be better if volatile. OST at first reminds me of kinda DL1 vibes. But still cons -Art Direction, colors and all other stuff look worse than a Nurgle -Bullet sponge everywhere -No scaling whatsoever -Difficulty is there just to be there -Dead Island 2 has a better gore system, and way better feeling weapons -Still no impact on melee weapons. -Asset flipping DL2, animations, boring gameplay, UI design, QTE's -Selling a DL2 DLC worth less than 20€ for 60. -No parkour points -All kinds of bugs for example in parkour it's quite obvious it's not polished

It's a really well done, high quality game, the storytelling is the best out of all Dying Light entries, but why I don't recommend it is that there's just not enough content to justify the price tag. In just over 30 hours of playing I have done everything that there is to do. (and I wasn't even trying to rush it) Unlike other entries in the series, The Beast doesn't give a reason to continue playing after the main story is completed. There is NOTHING to do in the game. Everything is unlocked, everything is finished, If you want to get the best value for your money - get this game when it's discounted.

It's clear most people want this game to be better than it is, but after all the bluster and hopes we're left a diet-version of Techland's original with a majority of systems clinging on from its poorer sequel. Despite appearances, the big marketing push behind "new parkour" is overblown, and its "beast mode" is a gimmick. More power to you if you enjoy that, but to me the game plays exactly like what it is: a DLC retrofit into a standalone. I'm not going to waste time on content for content's sake if the series can only offer a pastiche of what's come before.

i love the dying light series, but this one feels kind of a letdown. there's definitely an improvement from dying light 2, parkour and combat, though the same can't be said about the story. the game is so SHORT and for what?? it feels unfinished. maybe i had my expectations too high, but dying light 1 is still miles ahead. that said, it’s not a bad game, but I’d recommend getting it on sale.

Game series has great movement and parkour mechanics. Set it in a map where the few buildings there actually are are designed to make parkour awkward and fill the rest with big open spaces where you can't use movement at all

Dying Light: The Buggy Beast Just a short list of all the countless bugs me and my friends have encountered during 20 hours of play - Missions that cannot be completed - Missions that cannot be started - Game crashes - Fps drops after doing certain actions, not related to user's hardware - Night/Day cycle completely bugged EVERY time - Falling through the map - Indicators above other player's heads disappearing - Teleportation glitches - Rain indoor and many many more.. How Techland looked at this and went "yeah let's release it one day earlier" is a marvel to me. The core game concept is okay at best but the bugs just push it to being ridiculous, would not recommend as is

I don't understand why this game is on very positive reviews,it's written worse than dying light 2,the gameplay is messier,the optimization sucks i constantly dropped below 20 fps on low with a 3070 and a ryzen 9 5900x this game needs way more time to cook,also constantly needing to be connected to epic and the constant pop ups when you "disconnect" is annoying

Game is boring and story isn't well written, 80% of the game is go from point A to B, talk to this guy and do that boring mission etc. and I feel like they disabled fast travel so the game can feel longer. It's a crime this game doesn't have fast travel. Combat is bad and sloppy, and movement could use a lot of work. Game is really challenging and difficult, BTW. Ending is good, wish the whole game was like the last ~2-3h in the game.

The game is OK but id wait for a sale, this game is nothing special compared to dying light 2 all you get is new crafting gear, a car and different map and kyle crane of course, kind of a let down i kept falling asleep

It's fun, but isn't worth the full 60 euro price. The whole game feels like a B movie: an absurd plot, repetitive side quests, I haven't seen attention to details in level design. It could be a good DLC for DL 2.

Frame rate dips significantly once Castor Woods is unlocked. Strange, the opening "tutorial" section runs at over 100 fps with no issues. Castor Woods? frame stuttering, running a high end machine here too that gets 120 fps at 4k on Dying LIght 2.

Don't buy at discount it's not worth more then 10$ I'm shocked how it got so many positive reviews Map is small and boring Parkour is useless I spent whole game walking running Weapons are not as much fun as previous 2 games to mode Buildings are same and boring as hell dyling light 2 was a masterpiece when it came to map and buildings interior exterior both Story is ok but missions are easy ordinary nothing exciting untill the very end Hive and scavangers are both useless and nothing good is recived by spending energy destroying them I'm shocked truly am how granted the devlopers took us gamer and gave a half effort game It's a 5/10 compared to both previous titles

I would really like to upvote this game, but its god awful savegame policy won't let me. I had to hustle through a series of zombies to get to a certain point of the game, but had to close the game in order to attend to real life chores. When I got back, I discovered that I has hurled back to an earlier checkpoint and had to go through all that shit again! Nope.

Wanted to enjoy the game, I really did. One problem I had was the mouse in the menus just randomly stops working for a split second and feels horrible. The other issue is the combat. I cannot stand the zombies constantly grabbing me every 5 seconds. I'm mid attack animation and they grab me through it, damage me, and take my stamina. It's just insanely unfun. Then the other issue with the combat is the stamina runs out after fighting a single zombie. I might be able to deal with this if the grabbing was toned down by like 99% but I can't even attempt to enjoy it because of that.

Game is trash. Slow and clunky combat and parkour. Tiny skill tree. Zombies grab far too often and from way too far away. Bad design decisions persist from the first game a decade ago. The first Dying Light is still the best, by far. Just play that instead.

The world is literally dead. Not just because zombies - but because there is no life in it. According to the plot and cutscenes, survivors, crowds of soldiers, scientists, and so on are hiding somewhere around. However, you don't encounter them except during missions where the military is supposed to appear. Nothing happens by itself. Even the rare outposts with soldiers can stand for hours 5 meters from zombies, and everyone will be fine. For a AAA game from 2025, such a cardboard-like world is very noticeable.

The first game was great, but there is a lot about this one I don't enjoy and I don't think it was worth the price. It feels very much like a money grab by turning what should have been a dlc into a "full" game. The parkour area that I enjoyed in the first game seems lacking in this one with the jumping and running across buildings not feeling as smooth. Skills seem to focus more on the beast skill tree and turning into the beast. In one boss fight, I turned 3 times into the beast before I downed the boss. The satisfaction of the weapons seems less there than the first game. Something still feels off about this game vs the original.

20 mins into the game - close door - turn on power - Kill 10 zombies - flip a switch Yeah played this before, Wait for discount, you have played this game before Dead island 1/2/RIP DL 1/2 Its the same shit, runs well but thats a Lowbar Paying Full price for the same Mid slop is wild. "But you can go Zombie mode" from the 20 secs i got to play like that the, "Hunt" mode from DL1 was far more interesting, But if you enjoy mega punches go ahead.

every damn quest is just some overly long parkour sequence that isnt hard just time consuming and irritating i wish this game was better and didnt really live up to what i thought it was if i could i would refund it

While this game does a good job of tying Dying Light and Dying Light 2 together, ultimately it is more like a DLC than a stand alone-game. With minimal weapons, missions and NPCs, the game falls short of what it could have been. The game lacks the fun gameplay of the grappling hook of Dying Light or the paraglider of Dying Light 2. You spend most of your time either trying to solve frustrating parkour puzzles or running (or driving in the same boring vehicle) back and forth on the map. This game needs a major expansion patch if it’s going to come even close to its predecessors for quality and gameplay. I would say save you money and give this one a miss.

Disappointing. Techland released an unfinished game. Pulling Ray Tracing support at the last second. Apparently to be added back later. Shady. The HDR is a mess. No matter the calibration the bloom is ridiculous. The melee weapons feel wrong. They feel more like Dead Island than Dying Light. This very much feel like a DLC, and apparently it is a lot more like The Following than it is like Dying Light 1. I will play a bit more and may refund. So far this does not feel worth full price at all. A shame. Dying Light 1 is one of my favourite games of all time. This does not feel like Dying Light 1. Wait for a sale.

This is a good game at times, the combat is fun and there are lots of weapons. Why can't I reconmend it them because the DEI casting is so painful. The good factions are all run by small woman that every is supposed to believe are the toughest people around. They say this is in europe but at least half of all people are not european. We can not support games that do this crap or it will keep happening.

Dying Light 2.5 is a shameless Nostalgia bait. They didn't even try to create a story, the Antagonist is a Far Cry-esque cartoon cutout, parcour is clunky, combat feels outdated, "bosses" are damage-sponges with 3 attack patterns each, crafting relies on you collecting thousands of garbage items, dark zones are worthless copy paste dungeons, cars are completely out of place and exist purely because they existed before, etc. But hey, Kyle Crane is back, with a voice actor who's half asleep while reading his lines. Great It's a worse version of Dead Island 2.

As of patch 1.5 it is a tedious xp slog that is absolutely not worth it. You are not allowed to farm for xp as Volatiles no longer give xp. They expect you to spend hundreds if not thousands of hours to unlock all of the legends levels. They succeeded in making this game worse then VMB 2.

Inferior in every way to the old dying light. Even it's graphics are worse, you can count the pixels in blood splatters, the gore is just ripped off from the second game too. Skills are bland and boring. Also they turned Kyle Crane into a ''run when my eyes turn red'' anime character.

Honestly, big let down. the drop is quality from the last game is crazy Parkour is terrible. everything kills momentum. Skill tree is lame and linear. no choices to be made. constantly losing grip while climbing and falling for no reason, randomly falling off ledges you are already holding onto. respawning after death usually drops you in the middle of a horde with no ammo and you lose all of your XP. Walkie Talkie was a great idea. too bad they didn't use it and you still have to run back and forth to talk to people. This feels like a bad port

Parkour is a pain, swimming barely works but you're forced to do that too occasionally. Combat is fine, scenery is pretty. Dialogue is hard to get through. Thankfully bought this game on sale but still can't bring myself to finish it.

Was hoping they improved on their formula a bit... but not really... it's just a carbon copy of Dying Light 1 and 2. The parkour seems clunkier and the story is even more eye-rolly. I don't know how far I'm going to make it into this one. I already find myself getting annoyed.

First off, I love the series and I love all of techlands games. But this one is far too short and should have been kept as the DLC for Dying Light 2 that it was supposed to be. 25 hour game and the map while it appears large is innaccessable in about 30 % of it, plus the story is boring, and the mechanics arent as fluid. IS it good, yes, is it something worth your time, a little bit, = But it is not worth the price they are charging for it. This is a $40 game at most, $30 would be my recommended. I bought the $70 version to support techland, but I feel like itwas a huge let down

Story - so far very boring, im just doing the quest so to see if anything interesting happens - so far in 23h Playthrough - very boring Talent tree very redundant and almoust useless Enemys - oh holly molly - Thos zombies realy wana Hugh you 24/7 or Volitiles who are riping you a new one in 2-3 hits Map design - for parkour game to make 60/65% an open landscape - are you guys oke there at planing department ? i seriously cant recomend this game , complete mess

mid at best worth probably half the price its actually listed for you can tell it was supposed to be a dlc literally nothing is new to the game except you can drive cars and wall run same grind and gameplay oh sorry and the chimeras are new i guess. i wouldve liked to see a lot more changes and new things added to the game but beside those 3 or 4 things theres literally nothing. not a bad game at all but def not worth the $70 maybe $40 at best, skill tree is small and offers really nothing unique, parkour feels off and janky idk man just wasnt what i was hoping for at all

The same old dying light graphics look bit outdated they are selling a 2015 game add-on for a full price actually Dislike only cus of its price This is basically a 2015 game, 0 improvements yeah its fun to play but why bothering buying it if i can just play the first game?

I wanted to love this game as I have been a fan of the franchise, but this game definitely feels like a step back. The parkour in this title is severely limited due to the map being mostly open fields, marsh, or open land. The story and side quests feel half as much as the previous titles, and even the map feels largely dull in comparison to the first game. That being said, I still did have a moderate amount of fun in a playthrough but I found myself wanting it to end instead of seeking out easter eggs and challenges.

This game is more annoying than fun the constant grabs and enemies with guns have overtaken the joy of the game along with every weapon in the game doing the same damage regardless of rarity, having to hit a zombie in the head 5 times with a sledge hammer fells horrible especially when you are being horded every other mission. the beast mode is kinda lame as well in the way it feels like its the main focus of the skill tree

overpriced DLC save your money and wait for a sale. Feels like its lacking in alot of cool stuff they could of added to the game etc hives, custom cars, bigger city, different zombie types. its just lacking alot off stuff from the previous games that would of been good to see here. Looks amazing. parkour is great but isnt much different from DL2. story is okay, good to see crane again. But yeah, this is honetly just a DLC for DL2. 5/10 mid

It's not fun, that's the main part. The map design seems to forget that this series is a silly parkour them up as most of it is just open terrain with little verticality. When night rolls around you can either hide or get killed by the numerous infected that seem to spawn on you just as you get out of sight. The combat is meaty and has a nice feel to it, for a time, and then constant zombie grapples and bosses with needlessly large health bars sapped any love I had for fighting. On top of all of this, the story is bland and un-inspired. very much not the focus but a further disappointment on top of everything else. Wait for a good sale or just play the first one.

if you liked DL2 well... this is DL2 again it's basically DL2 with slower and heavier parkour that feels even worse map is nice tho DO NOT I REPEAT DO NOT BUY THIS FULL PRICE, 60€ FOR A GLORIFIED DLC IS A ABHORRENT SCAM, GET IT AT LIKE 20 MAYBE 30€ MAX also just personal note to everyone who said they got it for "free" with DL2: you didn't, you paid for a product and they gave it to you, they were legally obligated to give it to you, they didn't do it out of the kindness of their hearts please stop saying this and use your brains for 2 seconds

Skill tree was mid. Stealth was mid. Music was good. Gun play was OK. Parkour + combat felt jank. Throwables were awesome to use, especially the throwing knife. Grapple hook was terrible. Vehicles were cool. Nighttime chases can still make you wish you wore brown pants. Side quests didn't feel repetitive thankfully, but there definitely wasn't enough of them. Main quests were really cool, especially the later half. Kyle's dialogue still feels like he's a 10 year old child unfortunately, but I'll admit he had a couple really bad ass moments, and a couple super funny ones too. I'd honestly recommend this game if there was just more content.

If like me you were expecting more Dying Light 1 this isn't the game you're looking for. This is simply more DL2 with Crane returning to fill a void while the gameplay and story are very much lacking. I'm aware alot of people like what this is and I'm not saying don't play it or it's terrible, however, if you are a Dying Light 1 lover this game will not do justice and is much more in line with 2.

Terrible woke girl boss immediately. The map is tiny. The zombies are still to stiff they also grab you too much. Trying to climb the tower in one of the first missions for the girl boss is a nightmare. I want a refund trash game.

Sadly I have to return the game. I was hyped and hoped for a DL1 feeling in new disguise. It feels more like a Dying Light 2 and Dead Island 2 combo. Graphic are beautiful but it does not kick in the feeling that the trailers "promised". The beast mode is okay but i don't get it. I had fun playing it for two hours but i think in the long term it's loosing it's magic. (Combat feels off too) It's really good for people that never played DL1. I thank the developers for their great work and effort. Maybe i'll look into the game again at some point but for now i'll return it.

Game feels extremely sluggish compared to the DL1 and 2. And weapons feel like they have 0 range. Which is funny because zombies grab you 99% of the time. Game feels more like Dead island gameplay then any previous Dying light.

Great nostalgia bait. Ok gameplay it was fun but the story? Absolute dog sh*t. Spoilers ahead. WHY IS AIDEN IN THE GAME FOR LIKE 5 MINUTES? WHY TF DID THEY DO SPIKE LIKE THAT? I CALLED THE BOSS FIGHT 2 HOURS IN. THE SHERIFF? LET ME KILL HER. OLIVIA? LET ME KILL HER. WHY DO THEY FORCE THE STORY TO FIT THEIR ENDING. THE GRE BEING BEHIND EVERYTHING? STUPIDEST RETCON EVER, THEY LITERALLY JUST TOLD US THAT THE GRE WENT UNDER. It feels like they came up with the ending and then worked backwards from there. Unbelievable that they managed to completely ruin the story. Buy it for the little bit of fun, dont bother with the grapple.

For the amount of times that ive gotten stuck/glitched in a random spot on this game they need to add an unstuck option somewhere in the menu. Ive had to restart my game so many times because i would get stuck just exploring the world or even just looting a building.

Game is fun , pretty to look at , Gameplay feel great but if i had to pay the full price it is asking for , i would be mad asf. ( Ultimate Edition Owner) I cannot recommend because of the fact that it is just an overpriced expansion. Should have been priced like Dying light The Following / Cyberpunk Liberty city expansion then it would deserve all the praise... Get it on sale.

If you insist on trying it, I'd advise getting cheatengine, and setup a hotkey to toggle 300% speedhack for dying light. The story and pacing is stale and unoriginal at best, Ancient Rome told newer tales. I loved DL1 and really wanted this to work. But shortly before the downloading completed, I beat an Everdark boss in Nightreign. The contrast from fighting a giant squid demigod in the the evernight, making an ocean of the sky, raining stars and tsunami to our team, to fighting a generic zombie boss that rushes at you with the name 'reaper' is just too spectacular. Western 3A game companies really need to wake the fk up. Love me some jesters.

As a big fan of zombie games and loved playing the first to its a shame how this game has turned out with the issues with the day & night changes, to the overprice of crafting basic tools and shortage of loot and make it more a hit and run rather than a hack and slash with cool upgraded weapons this is due to zombies grabbing you 80% of the time The story is there and kyle crane the OG these to facts are great just everything else needs to be worked on. would not recommend this game at the moment until the issues are sorted

Performing terribly on a 4060 at medium settings with FSR / DLSS on. Unstable 50 frames. Old bug from DL1 & DL2 persisting of not being able to click anything on your other monitor while this game is open. Additionally, your mouse and scroll wheel interact with your other monitor while it's focused on the game.

Just an FYI, the start has a long intro. So that will eat into your 2 hour refund time. Game is dark, no not at night I mean It Is Dark Everywhere. You start with a flashlight, that doesn't work. It does briefly do a UV flash, which can stun zombies, but is useless otherwise when it is So Dark Everywhere. Trailers showed us using a flashlight, maybe that's a "skill" I have to unlock later? Seriously? To look forward to having a working flashlight.... Tutorial prompts are quick, miss it and you won't know what button does what unless you check settings or google. At least it doesn't come with the malware Deunvo, so that's a plus.

Horrible game, huge dissapointment after the previous titles. Parkour: garbage, clumsy af Combat: boring af Story: absoulte garbage horrendous world building, dog characters 4 aspects of the game: -walk across the whole map -go to the basement -fix the generators -fight chimera ITS SO BORING I CANT EVEN EXPLAIN, DO NOT BUY!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! Worst 60 euros i've ever spent, I'd rather buy amongus 50 times on 50 different accounts than to buy this garbage again. Huge dissapointment. Bober.

i cant bring myself to like these games. i find the gameplay boring. character animations look 2009. graphics are sub-par. looting items doesnt make any logical sense getting scrap off a zombie for example. yellow paint. predicatable. weapons break too fast and feel insignificant. first boss fight i quit and refunded once i realised i couldnt dodge and had to just trade hits and let the game balancing decide my fate rather than my input. i dont know this game just feels really boring to me. never liked the series. sorry. better physics and better presentation of items and balancing and i think i could go for it though.

Game itself is good, a return to Dying Light 1's combat and parkour. But the bad performance is too hard to ignore. I have a 4070 and am forced to play on Low settings WITH DLSS Ultra Performance on in order to get a playable 120fps.. Until this is fixed I will have to downvote.

i would recommend this in maybe a year when its finished this product was not ready for release in my opinion and feels too much like DL2 DLC although there are some improvements its still not the Dying light 1 quality i was hoping for - ran into a building and fell below the map - huge performance issues, playable inside the buildings but outside i could only get 30fps on low-medium settings with a 5060 rtx - bunch of visual and parkouring glitches

If you don't expect the vibe of the first Dying Light, you might enjoy it. But if you thought this was a return to that same atmosphere and gameplay… No, this is more like a DLC for DL2. If you didn't enjoy that, you'll probably get bored very quickly here.

I just can’t play this game, it feels like Dying Light 2 all over again. Same animations, same enemies, the same boring "restore the electricity"puzzles. Even the gore feels copy-pasted, and the zombies look exactly the same after every fight - unlike Dead Island 2. On top of that, too much time gets wasted looting everything, and it completely kills the flow. Refunded. It’ll probably be fine for newcomers and diehard fans, though. Still, it feels more like a DLC pretending to be a full game. Just look at The Witcher 3: Blood and Wine - the price and amount of content put this to shame.

Trash skill tree Trash parkour Mid gunplay Perfect gore Mid beast mode Trash bosses Trash map Cool but limited finishers Lame story telling Not woth for wait and price hope they improve these all evenly in nex game.

50 euros for this sloppy mess of a game? Fps are horrible, optimization is dogshit, got bugged a few times and had to restart, falling to my death instead of grabbing the ledge right in front of me and cables get bugged and connect to buioldings when you try jumping onto ledges. What a shitsshow, typical greedland though and charging cut throat rates for their games.

weak, generic and overall meaningless story. downgraded parkour compared to the first 2 games. extremely generic map area, not allowing for true freedom of movement in terms of parkour, which the last 2 games did. poor side quests. effectively useless skill tree that doesn't feel impactful. downgrades dating back as far as Dying Light 1. Absolute 4/10 at most. Horrible experience, except for the laughs i had with a co-op partner. Overall, extremely mid, and only worth it on an EXTREME sale. Go pick up Dying Light 1 for the price of a coffee, it's the best one of the series. IF you haven't done that, i suppose play 2.

This game feels (and at one point was) like a DLC for Dying light 2. Another review had already wrote everything wrong with this game down to a tee so ill just say this. This is not a BAD game, but it is not a 60 dollar game. Wait for a discount.

It just wasn't good. I loved the first game, enjoyed the second game. This felt like a bad DLC that they didn't release and wanted to make some quick cash off. If you played the Following, you'll even recognise a lot of location they've just re-skinned. The story felt incredibly basic, predictable and forced. It reminds me of my experience watching the Thunderbolts. Yuck. My advice, wait until it's on sale. Very heavily discounted on sale. There is SOME fun to be had, but the game's potential has absolutely not been reached. Not even close. The lack of fast travel or personal vehicles like in the following also made this game that much more tedious.

-) no choices in the storyline (boring one-sided) -) only about 10 missions for the main quest?? (~15-20 hours of playtime) -) not worth the full release price -) boring ass characters (side quests aren’t even worth playing: “collect this,” “run there,” …) -) Looting for what? There are no good items to buy -) small map and city, nothing to explore At least positive: -) Fighting mechanics are better than in DL2 -) Better graphics than in DL2

Overrated. Incredibly boring and one dimensional story. Slow progression. Good luck getting anywhere without being insta-grabbed 10 times and god forbid you fall into any bodies of water and Crane ends up sinking like he has concrete boots on. I have no clue why this is being touted as the best of the series because there is absolutely nothing to do but follow the main plot and rinse and repeat the same "Go here and fight a boss" mission, over and over again. Steam refused my refund so here I am.

The game needs more time in development, and the story was made with ChatGPT, with the dialogues written there as well. On top of that, there are lots of glitches and the map feels empty. People should realize that Dying Light’s core idea was that the game had two main areas, not just one. This game is overhyped, and its price is not reasonable at all.

WAIT FOR SALE this game is too short for $60 compared to the 1st and second one this is more a expation than a full game mabe if there is free dlc it would be good but compared to the first one this is small this game is closer to the following in size but i still feel like the following had more content

Mediocre. The beast dosen't hold a candle to dying light 1 and beats out 2 by mile. It's not too terrible but it's really bland. And definitely not the return to form or "true dying light sequel" people are claiming it is. It more like dying light 2 remix, same shallow mechanics with some improvements like gore, and parkour. But it is clear that the heart of the franchise, the original team that made 1 are long gone. It feels soulless and generic in a way I can't narrow down. 5/10

Devs need to stop forcing this toggle sprint trash on nearly every new game. it might not bother some people but it makes games unplayable and infuriating. It not F****ng difficult to not force toggle sprint get it together.

Sadly the worsty dying light we have seen yet. Dying lights main features were parkour and zombieslaying. Both of these feel AWFUL. The parkour feels sluggish and unresponsive, the map features only a few really high places with lots of flatish land. And then comes the fighting. Weapons need repairs every 3 fights and can only be repaired a certain number of times. This is probably supposed to make you want to run and parkour more than fight, but as stated above this also feels like absolute garbage, meaning the whole game just feels meh.

I was really excited for this game, but the bugs ruined it before I could even start properly. I had several ocassions with parts of bodies which were missplaced, floating in the air beside the character, totally destroing the immersion. In the first "Boss Fight", the bosses arms where also disconnected and floating in the air, making it unplayable. This in addition to the other bugs, such as raining indoors and the nighttime/daytime bugs makes this feel like an uncomplete game. I find this unaccetable and would not recommend people to buy this before all issues are sorted out like they mostly should be upon realease.

Love the game and the series but holy ballz we cant let games release like this, I have a 3070ti and at THE LOWEST SETTINGS I peak at 30-50 frames and the game feel so bad its insane this cant be how games release.

Honestly not a bad game, but what is killing it for me is the parkour. The parkour in this game is TERRIBLE. There are SO many areas throughout the game that I've found that look like they should be climbable but arent. You cant climb up electrical posts for some reason either, which is really off putting. Gameplay is otherwise solid, much better then the 2nd game and slightly worse than the original one.

More girlbosses telling you what to do. You people must have a degradation fetish to keep playing these games. And why do the survivors of the Alps look like the kind of people you would find on the side of a street in New York?…

First time I tried to launch it, it crashed my whole PC after clicking play. My fault for not downloading the latest drivers I suppose. Download latest drivers, click play and game actually launches but crashes after the first two splash screens. Try a second time and get the same result. Convinced these companies don't have a clue what they are doing anymore. Why is it so difficult to even get a game to launch? After further playtime I realise this is the most unstable game I've ever played. I crash every couple of minutes. Literally all I've been able to do is crawl a couple of meters and pickup a flashlight, after which I had another PC crash. Fuck Techland.

I can't get into it. After 15 hours of play I just want to play something else. There is no real story and its not keeping my interest. It's all about some guy who wants to kill another guy for making him stronger. You jump around from roof to roof finding the same old stuff for upgrading weapons and making throwables. It's a open world game that has vehicles and you punch out undead when you get angry. If it had stars one through five I would give a 2.5.

Worst buggy mess i have played in a while, dying light 1 and 2 had bugs but nothing as annoying as how stretching hitboxes this game has, if i had the chance to buy it for the first time again i'd let it go

Dying light the beast should have stayed a DLC instead of a full release. At full price of a 60$ its not worth, maybe at 35$ and the story is not good. Characters are ass, and all of the ones that are in charge for some reason are females LMAO. Graphic is good. Gameplay is ok, but previous Dying Lights are better. Side quest are not good. Some bugs also. I wanted to dive deeper in the story section, but i cant to avoid spoilers. Get it on sale for 35$ or less. Final rating 6.9/10

story is dumb, npc are annoying. They all sound like useless pusses. The story is simple and predictable. The game feels rush, beast mode is useless. Not sure what they changed in the climbing system, but it dosent feel right, also after you get to a certain point, its almost none existent, ends up just travelling by car. Idk the whole game feels weird and after the first part boring. Dying light 2 is way more worth it. Might be why I dislicked that one this much, since i would have expected it to be semilare in more ways.

This game is fun for about 15 hours and then, like most games, feels repetitive and empty. The melee combat is exceptionally good. The driving is exceptionally bad. The story is ok but I wasn't interested in the game long enough to complete it. I give it a 6/10, worth buying at $20.

This game is a nightmare and not in a good way. I don't know how they accomplished making a worse game than Dying Light I, which is from 2015. The gameplay is not fluent, driving the cars feels like shit. The game gets completly repetetive after 5 hours ingame. All you do is running from point A to point B. If thats for you, go for it.

Less than 4 hours in and I've already had to relaunch the game twice due to getting stuck. Can I walk over a pile of rubbish without glitching into it Techland? This review may change, but two game breaking bugs in ~4 hours puts it squarely in "can't recommend" territory.

It's like every Dying Light game but there's less variation in special kills, less visual damage on the infected and the game isn't a challenge, it is a cake walk game. If you want a challenge, play a different game. If you want to kill zombies and chill, play this, it's basic in terms of what you can do, atleast compared to previous titles, but over-all, I found it boring, predictable and repetitive. This is not my first time playing, I have played on console and well, same review, different place...

This is a major downgrade from DL2 Parkour areas are limited to old town you either walk or drive outside of it. "Dark Zone"s are a joke literally 0 volatiles inside you can just get in during the day and loot there are hardly any zombies inside Night chases are stupid you can outrun the chase by holding sprint and go in a straight line No new game plus, WHY? This is %100 not buy at current state

Game needed some serious polishing. Def a step in the right direction, however the dialogue was silly at best, immersion was a 4/10, not to mention the launch bugs with the memory leak issues.. overall not bad, not worth the money, this was a sad money grab attempt, though much better than the second.

Cons: 1.) Game is way too short for a $60 game 2.) Map is very small. 3.) Lacks a lot of content(skills, blueprints, vehicles, weapons) 4.) Only has 1 car of its kind in the whole game and the car mechanics are so bad 5.) More open land than towns 6.) Some weapons are not properly balanced and some don't make sense 7.) You can't upgrade the car 8.) Not enough side quests 9.) No Ray tracing 10.) Aiden(From Dying Light 2) has a better skill tree than Kyle at this point Pros: 1.) Fun with friends and the online I had no problem 2.) It is more challenging than previous games 3.) Story is okay That's pretty much everything.

The gameplay is fun, like all dying lights, but the DEI is cringe, the game is set in europe, yet for some reason every character is black or asian, wtf is this. Are we in europe, or africa, should only see 1 black and asian at most, not every single character, its fucking cringe just like the loser DEI idealogy.

Mindless fun, the story is still worse than the first game, parkour is fun, gameplay is more responsive but the traversal without a fast travel gets stale towards the end of the game, worth it if u're gonna play with friends, otherwise depends if u're a fan of the series or not. Also there's a tonn of fetch quests throughout the game and especially in the story towards the end where you have to run to different sides of the map which is annoying and also frustratingly boring.

Not worth the price tag its boring its small and it doesnt even stand close to DL2 much less DL1 i wouldnt pay more than $20 for it in its current state the map is tiny like the size of the slums in dying light 1, theirs not alot to do in it either. the vehicles suck and arent fun beacuse they have no customization like they did in The Following, The parkour is boring and slow, the combats buggy and weak, you cant get more health or do more damage like in the first 2 games, they got the guns right atleast. ignore the "positive reviews" theyre just fan boys who didnt like DL2 for no reason other than they are proffesional haters with no actul reasons behind their hate

Pretty fun and runs well but certainly not worth $60 for what is essentially a DLC sized game. This is worth $30 at most. Wait for a sale. Also the zombies grabbing you mid swing or as you're trying to run past them gets tiring extremely quickly. Hopefully this is addressed in the future because it makes for a pretty frustrating experience.

Really doesn't seem worth the full price. Game started as a DLC and you can tell. Maybe 30 would've been a better price. Hot take but I think DL2 was better. At least so far. Writing is still bad too but a slight improvement over DL2. Gameplay is worse IMO but it isn't terrible. just wait for a sale.

The biggest lie and deception I've encountered this year. The game is empty, has no map interaction, and feels like just add-on content, not the full game. I couldn't finish the game out of boredom. Also, the game is designed by artificial intelligence. I can't believe we're in 2025.

Falling under the texture: check Dying to some random box blocking your move: check Dying to pigeon spikes: check Killing the same zombies over and over and over and over out side the respawn: check Dumping all the weapons in favour of fists being the most reliable one: check Getting tired of opening 1001 boxes to get rags: check Loot despawning if you are not looking at it: check Zombies spawning in the spots you are not looking at: check Car exploding if you drive into puddle: check And so on and so forth. This game doesnt know what it wants to be. Tomb Rider? Assassin Creed? Days Gone? lets grab the worse mechanics from each and mash them together.

game play is 10/10 90% of the time but the remaining 10% is just unavoidable damage from zombie grabs, parkour that wont respond to my inputs half the time, etc. The story is unfathomably terrible. God awful dialog, constant commentary on what you do in 10 seconds, verbal yellow paint, literal yellow paint. all the characters somehow make me hate them, except for Jacob praise be to Jacob, Kyle Crane is somehow both the dumbest and smartest person in the game with his awful constant commentary and verbal yellow paint. definitely worth the $60 but wait to see what the devs do with the game.

It's just an over priced DLC. if they had just called it a DLC and charged like £30 for it then i wouldn't have refuneded! I bought it and played for 3 hours and there's nothing in it that pushes it past previous games. Wait for it to go cheaper.

The game itself is decent, and the gore especially is well done, but the lack of ray tracing at launch was a terrible decision. While performance is solid, it feels irrelevant when the graphics look a decade old by comparison. I’ll update my review to recommended once ray tracing is added.

I've been trying to play the game for a couple hours now. Audio keeps cutting out without warning. Disconnecting from network even with a stable connection. Would recommend waiting until game is fixed.

The volitiles hunting in broad daylight make it impossible to step outside without an immediate level 4 chase. The game is basically just running away while getting h it, in the back, until you're dead

I reallyyy wanted to like this game but..... -Random FPS drops -Stuck on random objects in a falling animation glitch -Random "Your internet connection has dropped" while sprinting for my god damn life (fixed by going solo but whyyy) -Could be were 10 years older, but the magic just isnt there this go around. Im not a fan of where we took this one (personal opinion, I know I know) 6/10 IMO at $90 CAD

No Ray Tracing on release. HDR is broken, probably because of the no Ray tracing on release. I'm so done with half finished releases, just freaking communicate with the audience before releasing it. "Sorry guys, the game is still in beta, you can buy it now if you want to play it unfinished. Or choose to wait until all works great as advertised." It's the same mistake as with DL2, RT also was broken on release. How can you make the same mistake twice? Lets hope this won't take weeks to fix. Weekend is around the door so don't expect it to be fixed these couple of days.

nice hot garbage the boss teleports just so he can hit you over and over and the barely able to walk "boss" can take sharper turns around the corner then crane himself also thanks for the 2 seconds of stamina when attacking and dodging but unlimited when running makes sense. Now put the fries in the bag and give up on making games techland edit: lol if you are standing on something and the volatile is under you they just teleport to you if they can't climb the place, Only good thing i can say for the game is that it runs good

No Raytracing at launch. I'll update my review when i get what was promised. Update 7th October. Just finished the game and all side activities, clocked in at 39 hours. The game is fine, but its not worth full price. Wait for sale. And no raytracing in sight. *Sigh* Its just better to wait when a game releases nowadays because theyre never fucking finished.

-Starts with infinite stamina so there's no natural progression of feeling more capable of traversing -Traversing where though? Dying Light should've stayed set in condensed cities. What am i gonna do, climb a tree? -Skill tree is a neutered version of its predecessors & MOST OF ALL they took away the exp gained by doing parkour actions to advance the parkour skill tree and gain more stamina. I understand we're half volatile now but that progression was my favorite part of the previous games, there's no feeling of becoming faster or more capable by doing those cool things that defined the series :( -The good thing is it made me look at DL2 in more of a positive light

It's got every stroke-inducing trait from the previous games and then some. Except for the grappling hook, somehow that feature is the only one that became vaguely reliable. The good parts are still there too, but they're buried under a mountain of input delays, camera locks and animation cancelling, grindfest crafting mechanics, breaks in flow where your character goes from flying through a fight to trying to parkour on a shelf (yes, indoors), downright unplayable night time, etc. If you like the franchise, my advice is to let them cook for now. This needs a few months of patches and basic QoL fixes before the fun can outweigh the rage.

Open world is amazing, but the story? Never playing it again. Might be the worst pacing I have ever seen in a game... Running (more than a few times) from one side of the map, to the other side of the map. Yeah we have cars. But they're slower than walking speed... Even mission wise it's go here, kill zombies and fix this please now or help me! Ending each mission with a cheesy voice line. I love dying light, but this was just annoying.

Super cool how everytime you die you lose the ammo you spent previous to dying. the controls are really sloppy too. Despite having a walkie talkie you still have to run back and forth across the map to simple talk to people. pretty lame game, could have been better

180+ Hours in DL1 200+ Hours in DL2 130 hours in DL:TB The only good thing about this game is pretty graphics after applying RTX. ULTIMATELY THE WORST AND FUNKY PARKOUR MOVEMENT EVER. The Map is so EMPTY, very EMPTY compare to DL1 and DL2. After finishing the story and side quest THERE IS NOTHING LEFT TO DO. TLDR: Empty Map Shitty parkour maneuverability Shitty movement Not close to DL1 Doesn't even top DL2 mechanics Worth $30 as a DLC

This game is a complete slog. Mission structure is extremely tedious. Story generic and boring. If on sale at half the price then maybe worth it, not at full price. I fell for the hype this was like the first game.

Nowhere near as good as the first. Map on DL:TB is terrible. The game has way too many open-ended areas that makes it feel like a running simulator, not a parkour game. The Chimera fights are repetitive after the first 2 and are a joke from there on. The skill trees are lame and do not go much into depth like the previous 2 games, and the vehicle is nowhere near as cool and intuitive as the Buggy from Dying Light The Following. I received the game for free, yet still feel as though I was cheated from the experience.

Not nearly as good as the previous two with map design, sure you can go in most buildings but the majority of the map is flat and doesnt provide parkour. Combat in the first dying light, and dead island 2 far surpasses this game. Cant recommend this game with the little amount of content it has. Maybe get it half off. I 100 percented all side activities, and campaign in under 20 hours.

Removed Framegen entirely from the game with promises it would return with 'high priority'. Since then there's been no word -- that was 2 months ago. Unplayable for me, personally. Cannot recommend until this issue is at the very least addressed

Super boring. Just go back to dying light 2. There is zero content in this game, I cant even finish it. I played 1 and 2 over and over again and I cant even get into this game. I started a new game on dying light 2 and am having fun again.

Game unsatisfying, plot horribly boring, parkour mechanics cool, just like previous games, but most of the time you run around empty fields. Fight mechanics implemented better in the game, but very little interesting fights regarding enemy placing. Night just useless in overall progression. All in all, it's like really cheap copy of previous games.

Tinnnyyy map compared to DL1. Bugs galore and doesn't respect your time. Story makes me think I'm in an anime more than a zombie game. I'm sure not all the positive reviews are bots but i genuinely wonder how many are.

Game is a drawn out DLC to DL2 that doubles as a running simulator. The parkour is choppy and the level design just doesn’t feel right. Overall it’s the worse installment of the series to date. Catch it well on sale if you want to experience it for yourself.

It's pretty much Dying Light 2, but with a terrain that is not nearly as fun to traverse, parkour is basically not necessary for general survival, only in quests, night is ridiculous because volatiles will always spawn in your proximity essentially cutting off all of your pathing, npc's dodge almost all your melee attacks so ranged is the ideal way to deal with them, but then it will attract plenty of runners, Don't get me on about the story, lackluster, predictable, and generally gave me no sense of accomplishment. Best part about the game is being Crane again.

This game is a bit of a conundrum in that my first 10 or so hours were really fun, but it fell off a cliff pretty heavily after that as the combat became tedious and the devs seemingly ran out of ideas. Ultimately, the Beast is an improvement on Dying Light 2 in some ways, worse in others but doesnt come close to the heights of the original. The game consistently misses the mark on pure fun in order to masquerade as a survival title and has some of the worst story, characters and boss battles I've ever had to play through. Absolutely worth £20 on a sale, 1000% not worth full price.

I've completed two 100% playthroughs of this game. There are certainly decent highs and extreme lows but what I can tell you is to wait til it's on sale. This is a DLC at best and certainly not worth $60. Techland should be embarrassed for charging full price.

Having seen what Dying Light is capable of in terms of the style of content in this game, I know they can and have done better in The Following DLC for the original game. I have seen nothing here that is an improvement on that formula. Only the opposite. At 60 USD I do not think this is worth a purchase.

I played 1, 2, and now 3. This feels more like a stretched-out DLC than a true sequel. The only real “new” thing is the beast transformation; the grappling hook and most systems were already in 2. Visuals are good but not noticeably better than 2. Night play is a plus—it brings back the tension from the first game—but overall I was disappointed. Pros Night gameplay is tense and fun again (nice throwback to DL1) Solid visuals and performance Cons Minimal innovation; feels like DLC turned into a full release Core loop is repetitive: run A → B → back to A If you’re here for fresh mechanics, there’s not much Verdict: Not Recommended

One oft the most borring story- and sidequests ever. Skilltree feels useless and pointless for the beast and the human. The last 2 boss fights are so borring too... every elitefight is only throwing some nades and mollys and then go in the ragemodus... u can onetap every human with the bow and the npcs are completly clueless. i dont know why i finished it but i wouldnt buy it again.

Definitely the weakest of the Dying Light games to date. Campaign is pretty straight forward and linear. Weapons all very samey and firearms are useless. New end game or New Game+ options. Buy on a 50% sale but not really worth the full asking price.

The map and the gameplay is good, funny with the coop and the gore. BUT, the story is cringe, boring af, and the design of the characters are disgusting, all the survivors has the antifa looking, not a single one looks like a normal person, all of them are blacks with rastas and afros, ugly women with lesbian haircuts, gays, and progressive jws, all of them with the merchant accent and one of them literally repeating every 5 minutes about he wants jwish food. Another game with wasted potential, immersion totally destroyed by the character designers and writers, another lesson about not buying full price games.

played all 3 games - 1 is still the best game by far. 2 was floaty and unrealistic but i thought that made it fun. this game is a middle ground between both but that just makes it bland in my opinion. parkour feels way less floaty but also not as crisp as the first game. the writing for the story is weak as well. definitely my least favorite in the series by far - wait for sale not worth full price

Absolutely horrific, Within 10 minutes of having unlocked CO-op, me and my friend got stuck on a bridge. 5 minutes later during night time, we were somehow able to hit enemies through the wall getting immense amounts of XP without even visiting town hall. At the boss fight in town hall, we were able to sit on the statue and beat the boss without him even responding. This game will need 'ALOT' of fixes.

Compared to previous games, the combat feels less fun and the parkour more like the worst parts of AC. They seem to be putting all their effort into making the gore more photo realistic, instead of iterating on the weapons and combat. A game like this shouldn't be so worried about players experiencing things in the 'wrong' order. Just give us some fun weapons, some fun enemies, and let us run wild!

I'm gonna be honest, playing Dying Light: The Beast It feels extremely rushed (I'm aware it probably wasn't). I feel we could've kept parkour and combat skill trees as a mechanic, especially with such a small map it probably isn't small; it just feels empty to me compared to 1 and 2. I feel the game isn't worth 59.99, though my opinion could change later on with free updates. I still think it's very fun alone or without friends. I'd rate the game a 6.5/10 (So far). I feel if the game has DLC in the future they shouldn't be too expensive due to the already some what large price tag (depends on the length in between each DLC. (All of my opinions are subject to change in the future)

I'm trying really hard to give this game a chance but I'm getting hit with so many small issues it's getting annoying.The longer I play the less FPS I get, the streets are wider making it harder to parkour, the walls you can climb are awkward and sometimes you can't climb walls that look climbable, the zombies can grab you from far away very easily, I'm having an issue where sometimes my keyboard inputs are delayed by a few seconds. This isn't a terrible game but man these little issues are really starting to get to me to the point where I cannot recommend this game in it's current state. Even without the bugs or errors I'm just not as impressed as the original Dying Light, 6.5/10

The game has not crashed or had any noticeable immersion or game breaking bugs. This however, should not be considered a positive and should be more of a minimum standard again when releasing a full title. The reason I can not recommend this game is the price point. I never owned the previous titles, so I'm not effected by that drama. The game feels like it's lacking depth, content, and is just barely big enough to not be a DLC. It is fun to play for a solo player, although traversing all the long distances gets a little tiresome. I would definitely wait for a sale.

things that made me dislike dl2 that i thought would be better in dl3 but isnt. - grapple hook - combat - weapons - blueprints - abilities ( not including beast ) all the things are TERRIBLE. like no improvement from dl2 whatsoever. given that it just come out, theres still hope.

Getting volatiles spawned right on top of you in an open area during a scripted encounter is just stupid and bad game design, not to mention they're spawning in broad daylight. Parkour and climbing is slow and cumbersome. Story is mid tier at best. Edit: Turns out the devs screwed up one of the most important mechanics and the day-night cycle doesn't actually change the environment because of a bug.

Of all the games I've played, this is certainly one of them. I've never regretted buying a game more than I have with this one. The story was awful, obviously written with Aiden in mind as the protag, but last minute panic had them just try and throw the entirety of dying light 1 at the wall and see what stuck. Vehicles feel awful to drive, the buggy did so well in the following because it was YOURS, fully customizeable, not some paper mache truck that breaks after hitting like 10 zombies. The combat was ok, but still lacking compared to even DL 2 imo. They should have stuck with what was leaked, not whatever this is.

Huge improvement to visuals and sound.Especially the music and the gore effects.BUT.Many bugs.Very small skill tree with no imagination.Getting xp is very hard and game forces you to do missions to level up.Experience and leveling system in the first game was much more interesting and rewarding.Gameplay and combat feels sluggish and hollow.Also HDR is bugged and makes nights darker than my morning coffee.I love the franchise but until Techland fixes most issues i don't recommend it.So far feels like DL 1.5.Needs huge work to be called a direct sequel to the first installment. For your information i pre-ordered this game and i regretted it bitterly.Not worth the price.

Honestly, this game is fun and exciting at times, but its just too short to justify the 60€ price tag (beat it in 22 hours including most of the side content). As a dying light fan that has waited for this game for a very long time, I have to say: I am dissapointed

Sadly nothing will ever measure up to the first Dying Light. For this game, everything you do is just obnoxious. Combat is slow and aggravating, with literally every zombie you come across grabbing and biting you, making encounters a slog. It's also far too easy and more likely you'll kill yourself more than enemies with your explosives. Lastly, buildings and such are too far spaced and awkward, making trying to parkour around and through areas a lot of stops and detours. There's just no good flow to the game and just not enjoying in my opinion.

Don't buy this game unless you love extremely slow, and painfully clunky gameplay. Hits barely register half the time, and your character moves way slower than any of the enemies. Most of the attacks cannot be dodged. You'll get used to the death screen.

the game still isn't ready the controls feel sloppy and the day and night cycle doesn't work i am very disappointed with this game had more fun with dying light 2 then this. 09/21/25: god this game is such a buggy piece of trash i got so mad that i broke my controller, i am done with this game going to contact steam and see if i can get my money bad such a disappointment 9/26/25 game is still a piece of junk am so done with it, i am so disappointed with this team on not delivering a game worth playing i truly hate this game

the servers constantly disconnect and even tho im playing in single playing im always getting popup notifications interrupting gameplay letting me know. It's severely annoying. IDGAF if the servers disconnect. stop telling me. I'm not playing co-op so i shouldnt be notified anyway. Remove the stupid notification so. It's killed me 3 times now in combat.

Really don't understand how this game has such a good score. The removal of fast travel is a huge pain in the backside. You get stuck at places at night and have nothing to do. The game wants to lock up the mouse so you can't even click stuff outside of the game when you alt tab out. So you are just stuck looking at a night in the game and that is boring!!

It's fun, beast mode is really awesome to use, however... The writing feels strange and unnatural, and some cutscenes are just disorienting with the shaky cam. RPG mechanics dumbed down even further, very very few skills in the game. No more EXP types, just EXP. The game is definitely not worth $60, maybe more like $30. I would only recommend on sale, or if you're already a huge fan of the Dying Light series.

This is clearly a DLC that turned into a money grab. You can go to the main menu and load your single save file. It will not allow you to roll back to a previous save. I got caught unprepared for a boss fight and there was no way to start from a previous save. I would have had to start the entire game over That is game breaking for me. I was really hoping this would take us back to the first game but I can see that will never happen. I am refunding the game. They should have just kept this a DLC its not even worth the 60 or 70 dollars we are paying. Clear cash grab by Techland and Co.

Dont let the intro fool you it takes a long time to finish it so you cant refund after its the same bullshit fake-rpg tankiness that dying light 2 was the game run at 40-50 fps with fsr on because of memory leak and gradual shader compilation

can't change keybindes to what I want and when I assign E,Q,1,3 keys it takes me to a different tab and why is it that when I hit a zombie below me my character hits it very slow? AND F BITERS ALWAYS GRABBING ME!!! and opening weapon wheel while running makes me stand in my place?! GREAT DESIGN If you already have the game then play it, but I wouldn't recommend anyone to buy it.

This game is so bad, this is literally made in the same engine Dying Light 2 was made in. It's just a re-skin of Dying Light 2 no doubt and you can't even play the game properly without you having to lose stamina every 5 fucking seconds
